mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the surrounding macro regime.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3m change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following declines are not significantly higher than the unconditional sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above 4.3% from the current 4.1% base, it signals an inflection in labor market deterioration that historically precedes risk-off repricing in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as investors reprice recession probability.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.3% (0.2pp increase from current level)
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.3%, measured over a rolling 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS and HOUST indicate a broad-based consumer and housing demand contraction that jointly signals weakening aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS m/m growth < 0 AND HOUST m/m growth < 0 concurrently for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns following joint RSAFS/HOUST declines are positive in more than 50% of triggered episodes across a rolling 5-year walk-forward test, or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions with restrictive monetary policy, an acceleration in INDPRO above 2% annualized signals capacity overheating that raises inflation and Fed-tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as discount rates are expected to rise further.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS above 4% and INDPRO growth exceeding the 2% trigger, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above 3.0% triggers heightened expectations of restrictive Fed policy, causing rapid equity market repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following CPI YoY crossing above 3.0% are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) relative to unconditional baseline over a rolling 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in labor markets, defined by rising PAYEMS combined with falling or stable UNRATE, signals resilient household income and consumption capacity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations are sustained.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS m/m change > 100k AND UNRATE m/m change <= 0
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following the joint PAYEMS/UNRATE trigger are negative in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series component signal over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in HOUST over the trailing 6 months reflects durable consumer confidence and forward-looking construction investment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days independent of the prevailing macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 6-month trend and forward 60-day SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the sign of the relationship reverses in more than one non-overlapping 3-year subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 1.5 percentage points (a strongly positive real policy rate) while UNRATE remains below 4.5%, monetary policy is restrictive relative to labor slack, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL YoY inflation > 1.5pp AND UNRATE < 4.5%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy regime with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during periods meeting the joint real-rate and unemployment trigger are positive or statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across a rolling 5-year walk-forward evaluation.
